message:
fix(daemon): improve robustness with safer casts and error handling

Multiple reliability improvements across the daemon subsystem:

src/daemon/worker.rs:
- Replace direct 'as' casts with try_from() for integer conversions
- Use saturating fallbacks (unwrap_or(0), unwrap_or(i64::MAX))
- Prevents panics on overflow when processing large datasets

src/daemon/client.rs:
- Reduce max response size from 100MB to 10MB (typical responses <1MB)
- Add structured warning log when rejecting oversized responses
- Reduce health check cache from 30s to 5s for faster failure detection

src/daemon/core.rs:
- Replace .expect() on thread spawn with proper error handling
- Log error and continue if worker fails to spawn
- Daemon remains functional for non-embedding requests

src/storage/sqlite.rs:
- Add debug logging when msgpack deserialization fails
- Includes byte length for debugging corrupt metadata

These changes make the daemon more resilient to edge cases and
provide better observability for debugging issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
